name: Dontayvion Wicks
headline: Talented depth piece buried in a crowded Eagles room
body:
Wicks has flashed efficiency without ever earning steady volume. His 2025 line in Green Bay tells the story: just 46 targets and a 10% target share over 13 games, yet a 0.308 EPA per play and a 56.5% success rate that point to a sharper player than the box score. Back in 2023 he posted a 60.3% success rate, 2.11 yards per route run and 0.503 EPA on 58 targets, so the talent is real when the looks come. The problem has always been the looks.

Now in Philadelphia, the math gets harder. DeVonta Smith, A.J. Brown, Dallas Goedert and a pair of drafted rookies sit ahead of him on a run-leaning offense where Jalen Hurts rarely clears 3,000 passing yards. An ADP near 197 and a sub-30-catch projection frame him correctly: a late-round dart, draftable only in the deepest formats and otherwise a waiver watch.
